notes: The doctrinally load-bearing terms in 2.40 are abhikrama-nāśa (loss of effort begun), pratyavāya (adverse result / diminution), asya dharmasya (of THIS dharma — specifically karma-yoga), svalpam api (even a little), trāyate (rescues / protects), and mahato bhayāt (from great fear, i.e., saṁsāra). The engineering layer preserves all six WITH the critical commentarial restriction: 'asya dharmasya' is karma-yoga specifically, not effort generically. The engineering layer states this explicitly: 'Krishna here advances a narrow claim... all concerning a specific discipline (karma-yoga, action without attachment to fruits) and NOT effort generically.' The contrast structure (Shankara's 'unlike agriculture / unlike medical care'; Sridhara's 'unlike farming / unlike Vedic ritual') is preserved in the source pack and in the engineering translation. The 'mahato bhayāt' is rendered (following Ramanuja) as the engineering form of saṁsāric fear — 'fear of being inside an incident class for which they have no model.' Five commentaries with named traditions. The standard misreading ('every little thing helps') is explicitly named and guarded against. PASS with high confidence in doctrinal preservation.
